25 images · 8 screens · 21 sprint tickets

0. Studio Benchmarking — What We're Stealing & Why

We audited 6 award-winning immersive web studios to extract specific, transferable techniques for CareerNEXT. This is not about copying their 3D visuals — it's about stealing their engagement philosophy and applying it to a career transition product.

StudioLocationSignature TechniqueWhat CareerNEXT Steals
LusionBristol, UKCompanion character (astronaut) that follows user. Fluid simulations. Mobile accelerometer.Evolving visual feedback as candidate progresses (lost → confident).
Immersive GardenParis, FRPremium restraint — minimal 3D, maximum impact. GSAP ScrollTrigger mastery. Core Web Vitals.ONE stunning scroll-driven hero moment (score 54→87 with particles). Everything else surgically clean.
Active TheoryLos AngelesFull 3D environments. Fractured glass cursor effects. Multi-user metaverse spaces.Gamified exploration — mission map feel. Jobs as nodes to discover, not lists to scroll.
DogstudioBelgium/ChicagoAdaptive 3D mascot per page. Custom transitions (Highway). Sound design as first-class citizen.Sound design system. Visual career compass morphing as profile gets richer.
ResnWellington, NZSurprise-driven engagement. Eccentric WebGL. "You fall into the site."Micro-rewards: particle animation revealing "8 skills detected" after CV upload.
Hello MondayCopenhagen/NYCMicro-interactions: elements shift, stretch, react to cursor. Playful + intuitive.Every score card and job card "breathes" on hover. Subtle cursor reactions throughout.

Google Career Dreamer — Competitive Gap

Launched Feb 2025, US-only. Free, no login. Uses Gemini AI + BLS/Lightcast labor data.

Career Dreamer DoesDoesn't DoCareerNEXT Advantage
Zero-friction start (type a job title)Parse actual CVsMulti-LLM cascade parsing (95% confidence)
AI Career Identity StatementScore your profile quantitatively7-dimension scoring with radar chart
Visual web of career pathsConnect to real job listingsLive job matching with compatibility %
Gemini resume draftingTrack progress over timeStreak system + weekly reports + trend lines
Playful BuzzFeed-quiz UXServe B2B2C buyers (CSE, outplacement)Advisor Mirror dashboard, institutional reporting
Work in French / European marketFrench-first, RGPD compliant, EU-hosted

Strategic positioning: Career Dreamer is a discovery toy. CareerNEXT must be a transition engine. "What if Career Dreamer had LinkedIn Premium's depth, Duolingo's engagement, and Lusion's emotional design?"

The complete user journey
Step 1: Arrive (Landing) — she clicks a link from her CSE
Step 2: Discover (Onboarding) — she uploads her CV and gets micro-results
Step 3: Orient (Hub) — she sees her score, the journey timeline, and her first action
Step 4: Understand (Score) — she explores her profile shape and gets a fix plan
Step 5: Improve (Resume Studio) — she applies fixes and watches her score climb
Step 6: Explore (NextJobs) — she browses curated job rows and applies in one click
Step 7: Return daily — Career DJ + streaks + weekly report keep her coming back

Every step has a door to the next step. No dead ends. No orphaned screens.

Step 1 — Arrive (Landing page)

Emotional state: Curious but skeptical. Anne-Laure received a link from her CSE. She clicks it expecting another generic job board.

BEFORE

Current preprod: English default, static phone mockup showing 87% score, single CTA 'Analyze my CV for free', counter '2,847 analyses this week', no RGPD trust signals, no partner logos

AFTER

Redesign: glassmorphism surfaces, animated hero score 54→87 in 4s loop (GSAP), RGPD trust bar under CTA, 3-step explainer, dual CTA, EN/FR live toggle, B2B2C partner variant via URL parameter

1.1 Audit findings

  • Language default must be French — IP geolocation or browser lang. A CSE-referred candidate landing on an English page abandons immediately.
  • Counter "2,847 analyses" — must be dynamic from Supabase or replaced with institutional credibility ("Utilisé par des CSE, cabinets d'outplacement, et organismes de prévoyance").
  • RGPD reassurance block — "Vos données hébergées en Europe · Conformité RGPD · Suppression sur demande" under primary CTA.
  • Demo path — "Voir une démo en 60 secondes" for visitors without a CV ready. Best SaaS landing pages (Linear, Notion, Loom) always let you taste the product.
  • Dual CTA — one above fold, one after 3-step explainer (Upload → Analyse → Plan d'action).
  • Partner logos bar — even generic categories if no signed logos yet.

1.2 Disruptive enhancements

Animated Hero: "Score Theatre" Teaser

Replace the static phone mockup with a micro-animation showing the score climbing from 54 to 87 over 4 seconds. Numbers spin like a slot machine, progress bars fill in sequence, then a subtle particle bloom celebrates the final score. This is the Stripe/Arc landing page model — the hero tells a 3-second story of transformation.

SpecDetail
AnimationGSAP + ScrollTrigger or Framer Motion. 4–5s loop with 2s pause.
MobileSimplified: just the number climbing, no particles (perf budget).
Effort2–3 days front-end.

Sound on First Interaction

When the user clicks "Analyze my CV," play a single subtle "whoosh" — Dogstudio principle: sound signals quality before any result. Landing stays silent (autoplay blocked). First click unlocks the audio layer. Howler.js (2KB), mute toggle in localStorage. Effort: 0.5 day.

Personalized Landing by Channel (B2B2C)

URL parameter ?partner=cse-renault changes hero to "Votre comité d'entreprise vous offre CareerNEXT" + partner logo from Supabase. This is the Alan/Gymlib/Swile standard. Effort: 1 day.

Engineering
Route: / · Components: LandingHero, HowItWorks, SecondCTA, PartnerLogos, Footer
Dependencies: GSAP+ScrollTrigger, Howler.js, next-intl · API: GET /api/partners?slug=X
Prototype: landing_page_redesign_concept.html · Total effort: 5 days
Where she goes next
She clicks 'Analyser mon CV' and hears a subtle whoosh. The journey begins. → Step 2: Discover.

Step 2 — Discover (Onboarding)

Emotional state: Willing but guarded. She uploaded her CV. Now she is answering questions and does not know how long this takes.

BEFORE

Current: 5 questions before any result, raw variable names ('work_life_balance'), open-ended question producing noise ('maison a larmor'), no progress indicator

AFTER

Redesign: glassmorphism chatbot with progressive disclosure, micro-results between questions, 6-step progress tabs, EN/FR toggle, file reassurance text

2.1 Audit findings

  • 5 questions before any result — abandon risk spikes at question 4. Best onboarding apps (Duolingo, Headspace, Noom) give value BEFORE asking for effort.
  • Raw variable display bug — "work_life_balance, skill_development" shown instead of "Équilibre vie pro/perso, Développement des compétences." Trust destroyer.
  • Open-ended final question — "How do you define success?" produces garbage ("maison a larmor"). Replace with 6 structured options.
  • No progress indicator — candidate in transition is already uncertain; don't add uncertainty about when this ends.
  • Upload button — gradient blue-violet lacks contrast on white. Add "PDF ou Word · 10 Mo max · Vos données restent confidentielles."
  • No state persistence — if candidate closes and returns, they restart from zero. Standard in 2026: save state, resume where you left off.

2.2 Disruptive enhancements

Progressive Disclosure: Question → Micro-Result → Question

Kill the interrogation pattern. After each user action, immediately show value:

StepUser ActionSystem Micro-ResultEmotional State
1Upload CV"8 expériences et 4 formations détectées. Profil : Communication stratégique."Surprised, validated
2Satisfaction slider (1-10)"Score préliminaire : 73/100. 7 offres correspondent déjà. Encore 1 question."Curious, motivated
3Priorities (6 structured options)"Priorités enregistrées. Votre analyse personnalisée est prête !"Anticipation
4CTA: "Voir mon score complet"→ Redirect to Dashboard Hub with Score TheatreExcitement
UX Psychologist — Endowed progress effect
Each micro-result creates the endowed progress effect (Nunes & Drèze, 2006): the candidate feels they've already accomplished something before they've done anything substantial. "8 experiences detected" after a simple file upload = immediate validation. This compels continuation because abandoning would mean losing the progress they feel they've earned.

Haptic Feedback on Score Preview

navigator.vibrate([100, 50, 200]) when preliminary score appears. Native Web API, zero library. Costs 1 line of code. Candidates physically feel their score. No career tool does this.

Adaptive Tone by Seniority

If CV parser detects 20+ years or MBA: adapt wording. Instead of "Are you willing to undergo training?" → "Quelle part de votre transition souhaitez-vous consacrer à l'acquisition de nouvelles compétences ?" with options: "Minimale — je capitalise sur mon expérience" / "Modérée — quelques modules ciblés" / "Significative — reconversion partielle."

Engineering
Route: /onboarding · State machine: upload → micro1 → slider → micro2 → priorities → micro3 → redirect
API: POST /api/cv/parse (step 1), POST /api/cv/preliminary-score (step 2)
Persistence: Supabase onboarding_sessions · Adaptive: tone_variant field
Prototype: onboarding_chatbot_glassmorphism.html · Total effort: 5 days
Where she goes next
She clicks 'Voir mon score complet.' Redirect to Hub. Score Theatre plays for the first time. → Step 3: Orient.

Step 3 — Orient (Dashboard Hub)

Emotional state: Excited but overwhelmed. She saw 73 in the Score Theatre. She needs: Where am I? What do I do first? What is the full journey?

'Mon Projet — Coming soon' — a menu item leading nowhere. Communicates 'product is unfinished.'

Wireframe light: rich but 26+ simultaneous elements

Wireframe dark: full gamification visible on first visit

Redesigned Hub: storytelling-first orientation. Welcome context, animated score ring (73), 4-step journey map with 'Start here' on step 1, specific first action card, persistent bottom nav. Zero gamification on day 1.

3.1 Why this screen exists

The candidate finishes onboarding and needs THREE things answered: "Where am I?", "What do I do first?", and "What's the full journey?" The wireframes answered all three but showed everything at once — 26 information elements exceeding Miller's Law (7±2) by 3x. The Hub redesign answers those three questions with 5 elements only.

Gamification scientist — Premature leaderboarding anti-pattern
Showing XP (0), streaks (0), and levels (Level 1) on first visit is like showing a marathon leaderboard to someone who just bought running shoes. Gamification appears on Day 2, after the candidate has completed their first action and earned something. This creates the endowment effect: having earned XP, they're motivated to earn more. Day 1 = orientation. Day 2+ = gamification.

Mode 1: First visit — 5 elements, zero gamification

  1. Welcome + context — "We've analyzed your CV. Your profile is oriented toward Strategic Communication with 8 experiences and 4 degrees."
  2. Score banner — animated ring (0→73), one sentence ("3 improvements could bring you to 85").
  3. Journey map — 4 steps: Resume Studio ("Start here") → NextJobs (12 matches) → Applications → Interview Prep.
  4. First action card — "Fix your top 3 CV issues" with specific keywords + time estimate. Single CTA.
  5. Bottom nav — Hub / Resume (73) / Jobs (12) / Coach. Persistent.

Mode 2: Return visit (Day 2+)

Same layout, evolved: greeting changes ("Day 12 of your transition"), topbar gains streak/XP/level pills, score shows trend arrow, journey map has dynamic badges, next mission updates via waterfall logic.

Mission selection waterfall

PriorityConditionMission
1Score < 70 AND no quick fixes appliedFix your top 3 CV issues
2Score ≥ 70 AND job_views < 10Explore your first 10 matching jobs
3Saved > 0 AND applications = 0Send your first application
4Applications > 0 AND score stagnant 14dUpdate your CV with new keywords
5All above completedReview today's new matches
Engineering
Route: /hub (default post-onboarding) · Max-width: 520px (story, not dashboard)
Components: TopBar, WelcomeBanner, ScoreBanner, JourneyMap, NextMission, BottomNav
Prototype: dashboard_hub_redesign.html · Full spec: Dashboard Hub Spec doc (12 tickets) · Total effort: 10 days
Where she goes next
She clicks 'Go to Resume Studio' on the first action card. Or clicks the score banner for details. Or taps 'Jobs (12)' in bottom nav. Every path leads forward.

Step 4 — Understand (Resume Score — The Full Sub-Score Analysis

BEFORE

Current: Overall 73, radar (truncated labels), 6 sub-score cards, Quick Fixes, Experience Stats ('0 Promotions'), Market Intelligence, Detailed Breakdowns — all at once

AFTER

Redesign: Score Theatre 4.5s theatrical reveal → progressive disclosure. Benchmarking line, personalized Quick Fixes, exit CTAs, context-aware stats

5.1 Audit findings

  • No progressive disclosure — radar + 7 sub-scores + Quick Fixes + Market Intelligence + Experience Stats all at once. Overwhelming.
  • "Manager Pass: 28" with no context — candidate sees a low number and feels anxiety. Needs: what it measures, why it's low, how to fix it.
  • Generic Quick Fixes — "Add more industry-relevant keywords" is blog-level advice. The AI has analyzed the CV and knows the sector — it should say: "Add 'stratégie d'influence', 'relations institutionnelles', and 'pilotage budgétaire' — found in 78% of matching job descriptions."
  • No exit CTAs — page is a dead end. Needs: "Améliorer mon CV" / "Voir les offres compatibles" / "Télécharger mon rapport."
  • Truncated radar labels — "ger Pass", "Market I" are illegible.
  • "0 Promotions" shown to a consultant — insensitive to non-traditional careers. System should detect career type and adapt.

5.2 Score Theatre — The Main Event

The single highest-impact UX change in the entire product. First-time score reveal plays as a 4.5-second theatrical animation:

TimeVisualSound (opt-in)Haptic (mobile)
0–1sCentered circle pulsing gently. "Analyse en cours..."Low whoosh building—
1–2sNumber spinning rapidly (slot machine effect)Whoosh intensifies—
2–3.5sDecelerates. Overshoots 76. Bounces back to 73.Whoosh peaks—
3.5–4sCircle fills with gradient. Subtle particle burst."Ding" — clear bellvibrate([50,30,50,30,200])
4–4.5sQualifying phrase: "Votre CV est dans la moyenne. 3 améliorations rapides → 85."Silence—
4.5s+CTAs: "Voir le détail" + "Améliorer maintenant"——

Re-visit: Plays ONLY on first view. Return visits: score fades in directly (0.5s). GSAP timeline or Framer Motion. 2–3 days effort.

5.3 Every sub-score analyzed

Each card answers: What does this measure? Why is mine this number? What specific action fixes it?

90
Profile Quality
Measures: Completeness: experiences, education, skills, languages, structure coherence.
Why 90: 8 experiences, 4 degrees, 12 skills, 3 languages. Rich and well-structured.
Fix: No action needed — excellent. ✓
61
Market Readiness
Measures: Alignment with current demand: trending skills, salary expectations, geographic match.
Why 61: Core skills stable but not trending. Market demands digital-first skills (SEA/SEO, automation, analytics) absent from CV.
Fix: Add 'digital strategy', 'data-driven communication', 'social media analytics' — in 67% of Director postings in IDF.
73
Readability
Measures: 6-second scan quality: clear headers, consistent format, bullet structure, appropriate length.
Why 73: Summary too long (4 lines). Some bullets start with nouns instead of action verbs. Date format inconsistent.
Fix: Shorten summary to 2 lines. Start every bullet with action verb. Standardize dates to MM/YYYY.
87
ATS Pass
Measures: Keyword match against job descriptions, format compatibility, standard section headers.
Why 87: Good keyword density. PDF format compatible. Minor gaps in trending terms.
Fix: Add 'institutional strategy' + 'public relations' — could push above 92%. In 78% of matching jobs.
41
Human Pass
Measures: 30-second human scan: unique value proposition, storytelling, personal brand, memorable achievements.
Why 41: Reads as responsibilities, not achievements. No quantified results. Generic summary.
Fix: 'Managed partnerships' → 'Secured 12 partnerships generating €2.3M funding, +18% YoY.' = +15 to score.
43
Recruiter Pass
Measures: 10-second recruiter scan: seniority signals, industry terms, career progression clarity.
Why 43: Titles don't signal seniority. 'Portage salarial' confuses recruiters. Trajectory appears lateral.
Fix: Reframe: 'Directrice Communication (15+ ans)'. Add 'Career Highlights' section above experience.
28
Manager Pass
Measures: Detailed review: quantified impact, leadership evidence, budget/team/results alignment.
Why 28: Zero metrics in entire CV. No budgets, team sizes, ROI. Manager sees activities, no impact. #1 problem.
Fix: HIGHEST PRIORITY: Add to 3 recent roles — team size, budget, revenue. 'Piloted €500K budget, 23% cost reduction.'

5.4 Quick Fixes — Personalized, not generic

FixGeneric (current)Personalized (target)
#1"Add industry keywords""Add 'institutional strategy', 'public relations', 'budget management' — in 78% of sector jobs. Current match: 62%."
#2"Use action verbs""5 of 12 bullets start with nouns. 'Management of partnerships' → 'Led partnership development across 8 institutional partners.' Here are your 5: [list]"
#3"Quantify achievements""3 recent roles have 0 numbers. Add budget (€500K?), team size (6?), attendance (2,000?). Managers spend 4.2x longer on CVs with metrics."

5.5 Additional enhancements

Anonymized benchmarking: "Parmi 234 professionnels Communication en IDF avec 15+ ans, votre score vous place dans le top 35%." Only if cohort > 50. Strava/Finary/Oura competitive dynamic.

Before/After radar: On re-score, two superimposed radars — old in gray, new in color. Green "+X" on improved axes, red "−X" on regressions. Single most powerful retention mechanic.

PDF Score Report: One-click generation: score + radar + fixes + action plan. Branded with partner logo. B2B2C proof-of-value artifact.

Experience Stats context: "0 Promotions" hidden for consultant profiles. Reframed: "Independent consultant — breadth across 8 organizations."

Engineering
Route: /resume-score/:cv_id · Score Theatre: GSAP timeline, localStorage first-visit flag
Sub-scores: Single <SubScoreCard /> component, data-driven from cv_scores
Personalized fixes: AI generates from cv_parser + job_matches keyword analysis
Total effort: 10 days (theatre 3d, sub-scores 2d, fixes 2d, benchmarking 2d, PDF 1d)

5.6 Full page visualization — the 4-chapter scroll story

This is what the dev team builds. A single scrollable page with 4 chapters, each fitting in one viewport. The candidate never sees more than one concept at a time.

Resume Score redesign: Chapter 1 (score hero + benchmarking) → Chapter 2 (profile shape as horizontal bars, ordered high→low, click to expand) → Chapter 3 (3 levers ordered by impact: +17, +8, +5 pts, with before/after examples, total 73→88 in 45 min) → Chapter 4 (action CTAs + supplementary data for analytical candidates)

NLP Cognitivist — The 6-layer information architecture
Each chapter resolves ONE question before the next appears. Layer 1 (anchor): "73/100." Layer 2 (context): "Top 35%." Layer 3 (shape): bars show strengths high, gaps low — gestalt before numbers. Layer 4 (diagnosis): lever cards with specific actions. Layer 5 (plan): time, effort, projected result. Layer 6 (action): 3 exit CTAs. The candidate leaves with a to-do list, not a depression. Key rule: each layer fits in one viewport. No layer requires scrolling to understand.
UX Psychologist — Anxiety→agency transformation
Three mechanisms at work. Positive-first framing: Profile Quality (90) and ATS Pass (87) shown at the top — the candidate enters the shape view feeling "I have things going for me" before seeing Manager Pass (28). Agency: every gap becomes a project — "Manager Pass 28. Add metrics to 3 roles. 15 min. Expected: 28→50." Social proof: "Top 35%" reframes 73 from absolute judgment to competitive position.
Engineering — Score page rebuild
Components: <ScoreHero />, <BenchmarkLine />, <ProfileShapeBars /> (7 bars, click-expandable), <ImprovementPlan /> (3 lever cards), <ActionCTAs />, <SupplementaryData />
Scroll: CSS scroll-snap-type: y proximity for chapter-to-chapter flow. Or intersection observer for GSAP scroll-triggered reveals.
Bar interactions: Click bar → expandable detail (CSS max-height transition). Strengths first (green bars), gaps last (red bars pulsing gently with CSS animation).
Lever cards: Collapsed (title + effort + impact) / Expanded (explanation + before/after + CTA) / Completed (checkmark + actual gain). Ordered by impact descending.
Prototype: resume_score_redesign.html (interactive — click bars and levers to see states)
Total effort: 10 days (Score Theatre 3d, bars component 2d, lever cards 2d, benchmarking 1d, supplementary 1d, PDF report 1d)
Where she goes next
She clicks 'Improve my CV' on Lever 1. Studio opens with the fix pre-highlighted. Or 'See 12 matching jobs.' No dead ends. → Step 5: Improve.

Step 5 — Improve (Resume Studio — The Improvement Engine

BEFORE

Current: flat skill tags, truncated summary, manual 'Score CV' button, no bridge to NextJobs, single CV only

AFTER

Redesign: glassmorphism, multi-CV cards with score + freshness, auto-score, ghost CV comparison, bridge to NextJobs, ATS optimization panel

4.1 Audit findings

  • Empty state wasted — "Select a resume" with generic icon. Should show blurred preview of a completed profile (Notion empty-state principle).
  • Manual "Score CV" button — scoring IS the value. Must be automatic on upload.
  • Truncated summary — "Forte capacité à fédérer des..." looks like a bug. Needs "Voir la suite" link.
  • No bridge to NextJobs — "12 offres compatibles avec ce profil →" missing. The two modules are silos.
  • Single CV only — a serious candidate has 2-3 versions (one per target role). Multi-CV architecture needed.
  • Flat skill tags — boring list with no job-matching context.

4.2 Resume Studio as improvement engine

Resume Studio is not a file manager. It's where candidates actively improve their CV for both humans and ATS systems.

The improvement workflow

StepCandidate doesSystem shows
1. UploadUpload CVAuto-parse + auto-score. "Score: 73. 3 improvements could bring you to 85."
2. ReviewRead parsed profile + scoreWeakest dimension pulsing. "Start with Manager Pass (28)."
3. FixClick a Quick FixInline before/after: "Managed team" → "Led 12-person team delivering €2.3M revenue"
4. Re-scoreUpload modified CVGhost CV overlay: 73 → 81. Green arrows on improved dimensions.
5. IterateKeep improvingPersonal best mechanic. Each re-upload = automatic re-score.

ATS optimization panel

  • Keyword coverage map: "Your CV matches 62% of keywords in matching jobs. Missing: institutional strategy (78% of jobs), budget management (71%), digital analytics (64%)."
  • Format check: "✓ PDF (ATS compatible). ✓ Standard headers. ✗ Summary exceeds 3 lines (may truncate in some ATS)."
  • Per-job ATS simulation: "For Directrice Com. @ Médecins du Monde: ATS match 87%. Add 'advocacy' and 'grant writing' to reach 94%."

Human readability coach

  • Bullet audit: Each bullet scored: ✓ action verb, ✓ metric, ✗ too long (47 words, target ≤25). Color-coded.
  • Summary strength: "4 lines (target: 2). Mentions responsibilities, not value. Suggested rewrite: [AI-generated]."
  • Section order: "For your target role, move 'Key Achievements' above 'Experience.'"

Freemium conversion model

Free tier (everyone sees)Premium tier (converts them)
Overall score (73/100) with qualifying phraseAll 7 sub-scores with explanations + fixes
Radar shape visible (sub-scores blurred except top 2)Full radar with all values
Quick Fix #1 fully visible (the teaser)All 3 Quick Fixes with specific keywords
Blurred improved CV preview — the conversion hookFull improved CV before/after for every section
—Ghost overlay + PDF report + benchmarking
Marketing strategist — The Grammarly model
Show the problem AND a glimpse of the solution, then gate the full solution. The blurred improved CV preview is the strongest lever: the candidate can SEE their CV gets better — they just can't see HOW without paying. The free tier delivers enough value (score + 2 sub-scores + 1 fix) that the candidate trusts the tool works. Then they pay because the remaining value is clearly visible behind the blur.

Ghost CV comparison

When a candidate uploads a second version, show side-by-side or overlay comparison. Old score in transparent gray, new in brand color. Each dimension shows delta: "ATS Pass: 71 → 84 (+13)" with green/red arrows. This is the Strava personal-best mechanic applied to CVs. Creates an addictive loop: modify → re-upload → see improvement.

Card "Breathing" on Hover

Hello Monday principle: every card scales 1.00→1.02, gains refined shadow, score badge pulses. Pure CSS, zero JS, 30 minutes to implement. Makes the entire list feel alive.

Engineering
Route: /resume-studio · Auto-score: POST /api/cv/score on upload success
Freemium: user.plan determines revealed vs blurred sub-scores
ATS panel: keyword extraction from job_matches + CV text comparison
Prototype: resume_studio_redesign.html · Total effort: 12 days
Where she goes next
Score hits 88. CV card shows '12 matching jobs →'. She clicks through to NextJobs. The AI Whisper nudges: 'Your ATS is now 93 — 4 new jobs match.' → Step 6: Explore.

Step 6 — Explore (NextJobs — Netflix/Prime Video Browsing

6.1 Current state

Current: static vertical list, binary 'Perfect Match' badge, 'Give me perfect' button, 'Select a job to see details' generic placeholder

Filters: functional but clinical. 'Filters are hidden by default. Click Filters to open them.' — that's documentation text in the UI, not UX copy.

Problems identified

  • "Give me perfect" — ambiguous label. Could mean filter >90%, or delete non-matches, or something else. Rename: "Voir uniquement les meilleures compatibilités."
  • Binary "Perfect Match" — 92% and 64% both get the same badge. No differentiation. Replace with actual percentages + color coding.
  • Static vertical list — no categorization, no story, no daily reason to return. A list of jobs is a list of potential rejections.
  • Empty panel is generic — should show candidate profile summary ("Votre recherche : Communication · Paris · CDI · 60-80K€").
  • "Filters are hidden by default" — UX documentation text, not interface copy. Remove entirely. A tooltip or micro-animation on the Filters button for first visit replaces this.
  • No bookmarks, no email alerts, no saved searches — no reason to return tomorrow.

6.2 Target state — Netflix/Prime browsing experience

Redesigned NextJobs: Netflix-style horizontal scrollable rows. Career DJ 'Your Daily 3' at top with pulsing dot and countdown timer. Themed rows: Best matches (92%, 91%, 89%, 87%, 85%), Stretch roles (78%, 76%, 72%, 68%), New this week, Remote-first. Minimal cards: company brand stripe + title + match %. Details reveal on hover. Browse/List toggle. Quick filter pills.

Storyteller — Each row tells a narrative
Netflix's genius: each row has a WHY. "Best matches" = "these are yours." "Stretch roles" = "you could grow into these." "New this week" = "the market is moving." "Hidden gems" = "you'd never have found these alone." The candidate isn't scrolling a database — they're exploring curated stories about their career possibilities. Career DJ at top creates the daily ritual: come back tomorrow for 3 new picks.

Row architecture

RowLabelAlgorithmCards
1Your Daily 3 (Career DJ)Top 3 by freshness × match × diversity. Refreshed 08:00 CET daily.3 (+3 bonus)
2Best matches for youScore ≥ 85%. Sorted descending.8-15
3Stretch roles worth exploringScore 65-84%. Jobs requiring 1-3 skills you don't yet have.8-15
4New this weekPosted in last 7 days, score ≥ 60%.5-20
5Remote-first opportunitiesFull remote or 3+ days remote, score ≥ 60%.5-15
6Hidden gemsCross-sector skill transfer matches.5-10
7Trending in your sectorCompanies hiring 3+ roles this month.5-10
8Previously savedBookmarks, most recent first.Variable

Job card — 3 information units at rest

Designed following the NLP cognitive load research principle: for a card that's one of 5 visible in a row, the limit is 3-4 information units. Beyond that, working memory saturates and candidates skip instead of read.

UnitElementWhy this unit
1. WHOCompany brand stripe (gradient color + logo + name)Absorbed as single visual gestalt — the "poster" on Netflix
2. WHATJob title (the only text they actually read while scrolling)The "show name" — reason to stop scrolling
3. WHY MEMatch % badge (92%, color-coded green/teal/amber)The decision trigger — "is this worth my click?"

Below: One quiet meta line (Paris · 65-80K€). That's it at rest.

On hover: Card lifts (-4px, scale 1.02), shadow deepens, a thin divider slides up revealing contract tags (CDI, Remote 2d) and skill match bar (10/12). Hello Monday progressive disclosure at the card level.

Career DJ — Daily curated drops

Every morning at 08:00 CET, 3 hand-picked jobs. Special section at top of page: pulsing green dot + "YOUR DAILY 3" + countdown "New picks in 14h 23m." Engaging with all 3 (view, save, or dismiss) unlocks "3 hidden gems" bonus row. This creates the daily ritual (Netflix auto-play) + reward loop (Duolingo daily XP). Supabase cron + ranking algo: freshness × match_score × diversity_factor (avoid 3 similar roles).

Per-Job competitiveness score

"Pour ce poste, 47 candidats ont un profil similaire. Votre score vous place dans le top 20%." On detail panel. Only if cohort > 20. Strategic intelligence to prioritize applications.

One-Click Apply with AI cover letter

"Postuler en 1 clic." CareerNEXT generates personalized cover letter (candidate profile + job description → Claude Haiku for speed). Slide-over panel: editable letter + "Télécharger PDF" + "Envoyer." Closes the discovery → action gap Career Dreamer ignores. Logs in candidate's pipeline (feeds Mon Projet kanban).

View toggle: 2 modes

ModeDescriptionBest for
Browse (default)Netflix horizontal rows, themedDaily discovery, casual exploration
ListTraditional vertical cards with full filter panelFocused search with specific criteria
Engineering
Route: /nextjobs · Components: JobRowSection, JobCardMinimal, CareerDJ, JobDetailPanel, ViewToggle, ProfileBar
Scroll: CSS scroll-snap + overflow-x: auto. Lazy-load rows below fold.
Career DJ: Supabase Edge Function cron 08:00 CET, daily_picks table
Apply: POST /api/applications/generate-letter (Claude Haiku), slide-over panel
Prototype: nextjobs_netflix_browse.html · Total effort: 15 days (rows 5d, DJ 3d, detail 2d, apply 3d, list 2d)
Where she goes next
She clicks 'Apply in 1 click.' AI generates a cover letter. She reviews, sends. Tomorrow at 08:00, Career DJ delivers 3 new picks. The daily loop begins. → Step 7: Return.
Important: timeline is embedded in the Hub
The progression journey shown here is NOT a standalone page. It is the journey map component within the Hub (Step 3). The dev team builds it as an embedded component in /hub, not as a separate route. What we describe below is the gamification layer (streaks, XP, weekly report) that powers the daily return loop.

Step 7 — Return daily (Mon Projet)

BEFORE

'Mon Projet — Coming soon.' The biggest hole in the user journey.

AFTER

Redesign: metro map with 7 stations (Découverte → Signé), missions with XP rewards, completed/current/locked states, gamification sidebar

7 stations of the candidate journey

StationNameMissionsXPUnlock
1DécouverteUpload CV, complete onboarding, view score+125Onboarding done
2OptimisationImprove score +10pts, fix top 3 Quick Fixes+250Score ≥ 70
3ExplorationView 20 jobs, save 5, apply to 1+1751st application
4CandidaturesApply to 5, generate 3 cover letters+5253+ applications
5EntretiensInterview prep + 1 mock interview+300Interview scheduled
6NégociationSalary benchmarks + talking points+100Offer received
7Signé!Log contract + Transition Certificate+500Confirmed

Streak/XP System

ActionXPStreak
Upload/update CV+50Counts as daily activity
View score after improvement+100Counts as daily activity
View 5 job listings+25Counts as daily activity
Save a job+15Counts as daily activity
Apply to a job+75Counts as daily activity
Complete a Quick Fix+50Counts as daily activity
Complete a timeline mission+200Counts as daily activity
Daily login+10Minimum to maintain streak

Streak freeze: 1 free per week. Additional freezes cost XP. Creates the Duolingo anxiety-of-losing mechanic.

Weekly Transition Report ("Spotify Wrapped")

Sunday 18h email: score 71→76 (+5), stats (viewed/saved/applied/fixes), streak (7 days), next week's mission. Shareable with CSE advisor.

Supabase Edge Function cron Sunday 18:00. React Email template. In-app dismissable card on next login. Shareable as PNG (html2canvas) or PDF.

Engineering
Route: /my-project · Desktop: horizontal scrollable SVG. Mobile: vertical stack.
Supabase: candidate_progress + gamification tables
Prototype: transition_timeline_metro_map.html · Total effort: 12 days (map 5d, missions 3d, XP 2d, report 2d)

The systems that tie it together Systems

8.1 AI Copilot Whisper

Contextual nudge: floating card bottom-right, 1 per page per session, dismissable. Rule-based triggers: ATS < 65 → suggest keywords, saved > 3 + applied 0 → help with letter, score stagnant 14d → point to Quick Fix.

ScreenConditionWhisper
Resume ScoreATS < 65"Votre score ATS est de 62. Voulez-vous que je suggère 5 mots-clés pour votre secteur ?"
NextJobssaved > 3, applied = 0"Vous avez sauvegardé 6 offres mais postulé à zéro. Besoin d'aide pour la lettre ?"
Mon Projetscore stagnant 14d"Votre score stagne depuis 2 semaines. La Quick Fix #1 pourrait débloquer +8 points."
Resume Studiocv.age > 45 days"Votre CV date de 45 jours. Les recruteurs privilégient les profils récents."

8.2 Sound Design System

SoundTriggerCharacterDuration
Upload whooshCV upload initiatedRising, airy0.8s
Score dingScore Theatre landingSatisfying, clear bell0.5s
Improvement chimeScore improvedTwo-note ascending0.6s
Job save clickBookmark a jobQuick, tactile0.2s
Mission completeTimeline doneAchievement fanfare0.5s
Streak maintainedDaily streakWarm, encouraging0.4s
Quick Fix appliedConfirm a fixConfirmation ping0.3s
Application sentOne-click applyCelebratory burst1.0s

Library: Howler.js (2KB). Default: Muted. Production: €300–500 freelance. No career platform has sound — first-mover differentiator.

8.3 Social Proof Stats

LocationStat
Landing"2,847 CV analysés cette semaine" (dynamic from Supabase)
Score page"Les candidats qui améliorent leur ATS au-dessus de 80 reçoivent 3.2× plus de vues recruteurs."
Quick Fixes"147 candidats ont utilisé cette astuce cette semaine."
NextJobs"67% des candidats de votre secteur filtrent en remote. Vous n'avez pas activé ce filtre."
Mon Projet"Les candidats avec un streak de 7 jours trouvent un emploi 40% plus vite."

8.4 Advisor Mirror (B2B2C)

Separate authenticated view for CSE advisors and outplacement consultants. Candidate list (name, score, trend, last login, completion %, applications). Drill-down to read-only Score page. Assign missions ("Assigné par votre conseiller"). CSV export. Supabase RLS: role = advisor, scoped to org. This is the feature that justifies institutional pricing. Effort: 3-4 weeks.

8.5 Bottom Navigation

TabBadgeRoute
Hub—/hub
ResumeScore (73)/resume-studio
JobsMatches (12)/nextjobs
Coach—/coach

Persistent across all logged-in screens. Glass surface on mobile. Desktop 1100px+: consider 80px left sidebar with icons + labels stacked.

10. The North Star

Every career tool on the market amplifies anxiety.

CareerNEXT should be the first one that makes transition feel good.

The candidate uploads their CV feeling anxious. They see their score rising like a game achievement. They discover jobs as curated stories about their possibilities instead of lists of potential rejection. They track their progress on a beautiful timeline. They come back daily not because they have to — but because it feels good.

That's the disruption. Not the AI. Not the data. The feeling.

Sources: Lusion · Immersive Garden · Active Theory · Dogstudio · Resn · Hello Monday · Google Career Dreamer · Netflix UX · Duolingo · Octalysis framework · Nir Eyal "Hooked" · Nunes & Drèze endowed progress (2006) · Festinger social comparison · Thaler & Sunstein "Nudge" · Miller's Law (7±2)
